Welcome to the final writing session of 10x sales pages track 2. So this one is all about coaching the prospect to the other side. You've extended them an invitation to exit. You've led them to that door and now it's about coaching them to make that escape. So this one continues on what we've built in the exiting the gap workshop except here we're escaping. So there's this greater sense of energy and urgency. It's where you get to remind them of the stakes and reassure them that the risk of staying where they are is greater than in moving forward. You remind them that based on the new beliefs and agreements that they've accepted their current reality is incongruent with who they've now become. So on an emotional level they've already crossed an invisible threshold a certain point of no return. And in these closing sections you encourage the set of real world actions in other words buying and using your product that are consistent with that emotional leap and commitment they've already made. So here's what we'll be working on in this session to accomplish that. We're going to be looking at the upgraded FAQ the perfect for you if close the final confident appeal which is going to close the question loop we opened up in the first workshop session and then we're going to explore a set of bonus closes the risk mitigator close as well as the MVC QVW challenge close to just add a little bit of extra urgency to the end of your sales page. Now all three closes are designed to coach one thing decisiveness. They're just using different mechanisms and triggers to do it. But before we break down each one and show you how to use them to coach the conversion in the final phase I want to remind you that all three of these as well as the two alternate closes can be repurposed as closing sales emails during the final 48 hours of your launch and Evergreen funnel. And if you are writing an email sequence to go along with it you'll also want to check out module seven of 10x launches as the email equivalent of this part of the training. With all that said Mimi in the next lesson where we'll go over the first close which is an upgraded version of the classic FAQ. I'll see you in the next lesson.
